scope: Defensive audit of categories not covered in Phases 1-4: privilege-escalation (PRIV), rate-limiting (RATE), crypto/secrets (CRYPTO), observability (OBS).

context_baseline.do_not_reflag: Findings duplicating any item in phase-3-audit-prd.json or phase-4-audit-prd.json.

qualityStandards.test: All Phase-5 fixes must pass `php artisan test` and `vendor/bin/pint --test`.
qualityStandards.ci: Each Phase commit must land on main with green CI before next phase starts.
qualityStandards.no_breaking_change: Existing test mocks expecting null returns should keep passing; only widen behaviour at error-path edges.

PRIV.description: Privilege escalation, missing authorization, IDOR via shared-attribute lookups, mass-assignment escalation, sub-resource auth gaps.

RATE.description: Missing rate limiters, throttle-name typos, account-lockout absence, signed-URL replay, quota-burn vectors.

CRYPTO.description: Dead crypto-rule code, magic-link expiry, SSRF via tenant-controlled URLs, plaintext PII at rest, session fixation, key rotation.

OBS.description: Missing error tracking, static health check, swallowed exceptions, missing channels, unaudited admin actions, no metrics, no request id.

items.title: PasswordPolicy class is dead code — never invoked anywhere
items.evidence: All 5 password-write endpoints use Rules\Password::defaults() (Laravel built-in, 8-char min). Grep confirms zero imports/instantiations of PasswordPolicy outside its own file. Phase-4 HANDLE-11 HIBP fail-open hardening, the 22-password banlist, the 12-char minimum, and symbol enforcement all have ZERO runtime effect.
items.fix: In AppServiceProvider::boot, call Password::defaults(fn () => Password::min(12)->mixedCase()->numbers()->symbols()->rules([new PasswordPolicy])). This makes Phase-4 HANDLE-11 + the existing PasswordPolicy rule live everywhere `Rules\Password::defaults()` is used.

items.title: Magic-login URL in TenantWelcome has no expiry — permanent backdoor login from forwarded email
items.evidence: URL::signedRoute('email.preferences', ['user' => $tenant->id]) (no expiry). NotificationsController::emailPreferences calls Auth::login($user). All 7 sibling mailables use temporarySignedRoute.
items.fix: Replace with URL::temporarySignedRoute('email.preferences', now()->addDays(7), ['user' => $tenant->id]) to match peer mailables. Better: stop auto-login entirely — render preferences without elevating session identity.

items.title: SSRF via tenant-controlled azure_vision_endpoint setting
items.evidence: $endpoint = Setting::get('azure_vision_endpoint', null, $landlordId) is concatenated into Http::post("{$endpoint}/..."); the response's Operation-Location header is then followed by Http::get($operationLocation) with no host validation. A landlord can set endpoint to http://169.254.169.254/, http://localhost:6379, etc.
items.fix: Validate the URL: parse_url, reject private/loopback/link-local IP ranges (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16, 127/8, 169.254/16, ::1, fc00::/7), allowlist *.cognitiveservices.azure.com. Refuse to follow Operation-Location headers whose host differs from the configured endpoint.

items.title: Session fixation — 6 auth()->login() sites do not regenerate session ID
items.evidence: Each elevates session privilege without $request->session()->regenerate(). AuthenticatedSessionController::store and TwoFactorController::challenge regenerate; these don't.
items.fix: Add $request->session()->regenerate() immediately after each Auth::login() call. For impersonation, regenerate on both start and stop.

items.title: LandlordPayoutAccount account_number/mobile_number stored plaintext at rest
items.evidence: account_number is in $hidden and getAuditExclude() but has NO 'encrypted' cast — unlike PaymentConfiguration.bank_account_number which IS encrypted. DB dump exposes every landlord's payout bank account.
items.fix: Add 'account_number' => 'encrypted', 'mobile_number' => 'encrypted', 'account_name' => 'encrypted' to $casts. Write a chunked backfill migration that re-saves existing rows through the encrypter.

items.title: No APP_KEY rotation procedure — 7 encrypted-column families unreadable on rotation
items.evidence: 7+ encrypted column families (User national_id/bank_details, PaymentConfiguration credentials, Setting.value, NotificationProviderConfig.credentials, two_factor_secret/recovery_codes). HANDLE-12 logs explicitly cite 'after APP_KEY rotation' as known failure mode, acknowledging the gap.
items.fix: Add `php artisan crypt:rotate {--old-key=}` that iterates each encrypted column chunked, decrypts with old key, re-encrypts with new. Document in deployment runbook.

items.title: Lease wallet-adjustment route accepts any authenticated user — tenants can credit own wallet
items.evidence: POST /leases/{lease}/wallet-adjustment is gated only by 'auth' (no role:landlord,caretaker), no FormRequest, no $this->authorize. TenantScope binds Lease for the tenant by landlord_id — tenant can route-bind their own lease and credit wallet_balance.
items.fix: Add 'role:landlord,caretaker' middleware on the route. Convert walletAdjustment to a WalletAdjustmentRequest with authorize() verifying isLandlord/isCaretaker AND $this->route('lease')->landlord_id matches resolved landlordId.

items.title: WaterReadingController destroy/update have no policy invocation — tenants delete sibling readings
items.evidence: WaterReadingPolicy::delete exists but never called. Route gated only by 'auth'. Tenant under landlord X can DELETE /readings/{reading} for any reading under landlord X (TenantScope binds it), poisoning utility billing.
items.fix: Add $this->authorize('update', $reading) and $this->authorize('delete', $reading) at top of update/destroy. Tighten UpdateWaterReadingRequest::authorize to verify $reading->landlord_id.

items.title: Signed email-preferences URL silently logs in tenant — impersonation gap beyond AdminController
items.evidence: Auth::login($user) based on user id from signed query-string. Phase-4 AUDIT-1 hardened impersonation through AdminController; this second identity-switching path missed. SecurityLogger never invoked.
items.fix: Inject SecurityLogger; call logLogin($user, 'signed-link', $request) before Auth::login. Better: remove auto-login and require existing session (PRD overlap with CRYPTO-2).

items.title: InvitationController and TenantInvitationController acceptAuthenticated mass-assign role/landlord_id silently
items.evidence: $user->update(['role' => ..., 'landlord_id' => ...]) — User::$fillable does NOT include 'role' or 'landlord_id'. Update silently succeeds, role/landlord_id unchanged. Net effect: landlord/caretaker who accepts a tenant invitation keeps their role AND a Lease is created with tenant_id pointing at them.
items.fix: Replace with direct attribute assignment + save(): $user->role = 'caretaker'; $user->landlord_id = $invitation->landlord_id; $user->save(). Add authorize() rejecting calls when existing $user->role is landlord/super_admin.

items.title: Bank webhook + M-Pesa C2B Till lookups have no landlord_id scope
items.evidence: Webhook signature middleware authenticates the gateway, but findInvoice/findTenantByPhone resolve across all landlords. Two landlords with colliding invoice_number formats get cross-credited.
items.fix: Resolve landlord_id from PaymentConfiguration (per-landlord callback URL or short_code mapping); scope queries by landlord_id. If no landlord context resolves, queue to BankReconciliationQueue with status 'unmatched'.

items.title: LeaseController/PropertyController/InboxController use auth()->id() as landlord_id — caretakers create orphans
items.evidence: Direct extension of Phase-3 SCOPE-S3 same bug class. For caretaker, auth()->id() is caretaker's user_id, not landlord's id. Records become orphaned vs TenantScope by real landlord.
items.fix: Use $user->isCaretaker() ? $user->landlord_id : $user->id throughout. Extract Controller::resolveLandlordId() helper.

items.title: API two-factor route uses non-existent limiter name 'two_factor' (underscore vs dash)
items.evidence: ->middleware('throttle:two_factor') but AppServiceProvider registers 'two-factor' (dash). Mismatch silently parses as legacy <max>,<minutes> syntax or default 60/min. Web route at routes/web.php:95 correctly uses 'two-factor'.
items.fix: Change routes/api.php:34 to ->middleware('throttle:two-factor'). Add Pest test asserting 6th POST returns 429.

items.title: InvoiceController::sendReminder no throttle — landlord can blast tenant via email queue
items.evidence: Route::post('/invoices/{invoice}/send-reminder', ...) — no throttle. Each call queues mail; no per-invoice cooldown.
items.fix: Register notification-send limiter: Limit::perMinute(3)->by('reminder:'.$invoice->id). Apply ->middleware('throttle:notification-send') to send-reminder, send-receipt, ledger-email.

items.title: Notifications send/sendBulk/sendRentReminders/sendArrearsNotices unthrottled — fan-out spam vector
items.evidence: None throttled. SendBulkNotificationRequest doesn't cap recipient_ids array length. Compromised caretaker can enqueue tens of thousands of SMS.
items.fix: Add bulk-notify limiter Limit::perMinute(2)->by('landlord:'.$landlordId). Cap recipient_ids at 500 per request.

items.title: BulkOperations endpoints unthrottled and lack concurrent-action lock
items.evidence: All 7 bulk routes (adjustRent, terminateLeases, extendLeases, adjustDeposits, updateUnitStatus, updateMeterNumbers, updateTargetRent) live in auth() with no throttle and no Cache::lock. Double-click fires concurrent writes + queue floods.
items.fix: Register bulk-ops limiter (3/min/user). Wrap action body in Cache::lock('bulk:'.$landlordId, 60)->block(0, fn() => ...).

items.title: Inbox reply no per-conversation rate limit — landlord can blast tenant via SMS/WhatsApp
items.evidence: InboxController::reply sends paid SMS/WhatsApp on each call. Nothing prevents 200 replies in 2 min (harassment + cost-amplification).
items.fix: Register inbox-reply limiter: Limit::perMinute(5)->by('thread:'.$message->user_id.':'.$user->id). Server-side 30s cooldown via TenantMessage.last_reply_at.

items.title: No external error tracking — production errors only land in local log files
items.evidence: No sentry/bugsnag/flare in composer.json. DomainException::report() does Log::error only. Ops can't aggregate, alert, dedup, or release-tag.
items.fix: Install sentry/sentry-laravel; bind in bootstrap/app.php / Handler::register(). DomainException::report() also calls Sentry::captureException after Log::error.

items.title: Health endpoint /api/v1/health is a static literal — no DB/Redis/queue/Reverb checks
items.evidence: Closure returns ['status' => 'ok', 'version' => '1.0'] regardless of underlying state. Wedged queue worker or down Redis is invisible.
items.fix: Replace with HealthCheckController::index that runs DB::connection()->getPdo(), Redis::connection()->ping(), Queue::size('default'), checks WebhookDeadLetter depth. Returns 200 only when all green; 503 on degradation.

items.title: Schedulers use runInBackground() with no onFailure() — silent cron failures
items.evidence: runInBackground() discards stdout/stderr. A failing invoices:automate (monthly invoice gen for every landlord) leaves zero trace.
items.fix: Append ->emailOutputOnFailure(config('app.ops_email')) AND ->onFailure(fn() => Log::channel('security')->error(...)) to every Schedule::command call.

items.title: NotificationService channel-send catches swallow exceptions with NO Log:: call
items.evidence: Each catch calls markAsFailed but no Log::error. Twilio outage produces zero log lines — operators can't triage 'why didn't tenants get rent reminders last night?'
items.fix: Inside each catch: Log::channel('notifications')->error('Notification dispatch failed', [notification_id, channel, recipient_id, landlord_id, error, exception_class]) BEFORE markAsFailed.

items.title: PaymentConfiguration secret-key updates are NOT audit-logged
items.evidence: $config->update($data) on payment configuration with NO SecurityLogger / AuditLog. Forensics on a leaked/swapped key has no audit trail.
items.fix: After $config->update, call SecurityLogger::logPaymentConfigChange($user, $config, $changedFieldNamesOnly). Add Auditable to PaymentConfiguration model.

items.title: No metric counters for payment-success rate, webhook failure rate, notification delivery rate
items.evidence: Zero Cache::increment, no Prometheus exporter, no StatsD client. Cannot answer 'has Paystack been degraded today?' or 'did last night's late-fee run process the right number?'
items.fix: Introduce thin MetricsService bound in container (Redis-backed counters). Increment from PaymentCallbackProcessor, MpesaWebhookController, SendNotificationJob, ApplyLateFees. Surface in /admin/metrics dashboard.

items.title: AddTicketCommentRequest::authorize returns true for any landlord/caretaker without ticket scope
items.fix: Match ResolveTicketRequest pattern: resolve $landlordId, return $ticket->landlord_id === $landlordId.

items.title: VerificationController::storeTemplate has no role guard — tenants create templates
items.fix: Add isLandlord||isCaretaker guard at top. Scope property_id with Rule::exists()->where('landlord_id', $landlordId).

items.title: Api\AuthController::register lets anyone mint a tenant API token without invitation
items.fix: Delete the endpoint OR require invitation_token parameter that resolves landlord_id from valid TenantInvitation.

items.title: ImportsController::upload accepts any building_id without landlord scope
items.fix: Replace exists rule with Rule::exists('buildings', 'id')->where('landlord_id', $landlordId).

items.title: InboxController::reply hard-codes auth()->id() as landlord_id (caretaker bug)
items.fix: Resolve $landlordId properly. Add TenantMessage::where('landlord_id', $landlordId) check on route binding.

items.title: TenantController::ledgerEmail/ledgerPdf accept arbitrary tenant route binding without policy
items.fix: Add TenantPolicy::viewLedger. Authorize ledger/ledgerPdf/ledgerEmail/modalData/outstandingInvoices/refundablePayments. Add role:landlord,caretaker to routes.

items.title: Big route group missing role:landlord,caretaker on landlord management surface
items.fix: Wrap the inner block with Route::middleware('role:landlord,caretaker') for landlord/caretaker-only routes. Keep notifications/profile/help/two-factor in inner group.

items.title: Tenant API mpesa/status checkMpesaStatus unthrottled — STK quota exhaustion
items.fix: Add ->middleware('throttle:payment').

items.title: GdprController immediateExport/requestExport/downloadExport unthrottled
items.fix: Apply throttle:export. Guard requestExport with ShouldBeUnique on the ExportUserData job.

items.title: Account-level lockout missing — IP-rotating credential-stuffing bypasses login limiter
items.fix: Add account_locked_at + failed_login_count columns. Check separate RateLimiter::tooManyAttempts('login-email:'.$email, 15) (15 failures across all IPs in 1h triggers 30-min email-only lock).

items.title: Signed unsubscribe / email-preferences URLs replayable — no nonce, full TTL re-use
items.fix: Single-use opaque token in signed_link_uses table; mark used in middleware. OR downgrade emailPreferences to require password before session attaches.

items.title: PaymentsHub verifyAccount and getBanks unthrottled — Paystack quota burn + name enumeration
items.fix: Apply throttle:sensitive (3/min) or new bank-verify limiter.

items.title: Notification retry/runScheduleNow/testProvider/previewTemplate unthrottled
items.fix: Apply throttle:sensitive (3/min) to retry/run; new provider-test limiter for testProvider/previewTemplate.

items.title: 2FA recovery codes use Str::random and non-constant-time matching
items.fix: Generator: strtoupper(bin2hex(random_bytes(5))) segmented. Verify: $codes->reduce(fn($c,$x)=>$c||hash_equals($x,$normalizedCode), false).

items.title: Three different secure-token generators across codebase — standardize
items.fix: Centralize via App\Support\Tokens::secure(int $bytes = 32): string. Route all generators through it.

items.title: QueuedPaymentIntent::generateIdempotencyKey uses bare hash() — predictable if nonce is client-supplied
items.fix: Convert to hash_hmac('sha256', payload, config('app.key')) so derivation requires APP_KEY. Or delete if dead code.

items.title: Tenant-supplied callback_url forwarded to Paystack — open-redirect / payment-status-leak vector
items.fix: Validate callback_url host matches parse_url(config('app.url'), PHP_URL_HOST). Reject anything else with 422.

items.title: Bank webhook secrets are platform-wide env values — single point of failure
items.fix: Move webhook secrets to per-landlord PaymentConfiguration::{bank}_webhook_secret with 'encrypted' cast.

items.title: Log::channel('notifications') referenced but channel does not exist in config/logging.php
items.fix: Add 'notifications' and 'payments' channel blocks (driver: daily, 30-day retention) for parity with 'security'/'whatsapp'/'cache'.

items.title: Subscription tier change is unaudited
items.fix: Add use Auditable to Subscription. SecurityLog row for paid-tier transitions.

items.title: External-API call durations only land in laravel.log — no metrics sink
items.fix: Persist to external_request_logs table OR write Redis sliding window for Prometheus scrape.

items.title: Production has no lazy-loading detection — preventLazyLoading disabled outside non-production
items.fix: Add sampled prod branch: if (random_int(1, 100) === 1) { Model::handleLazyLoadingViolationUsing(...) } so 1% of prod requests still surface N+1.

items.title: No request-id / correlation-id middleware
items.fix: Add AddRequestId middleware; Log::withContext(['request_id' => Str::uuid()]) per request. Expose to Inertia/API as X-Request-Id header.

items.title: MpesaWebhookController b2cTimeout logs full payload including PII
items.fix: Replace with redaction pattern. Or extract just safe fields (OriginatorConversationID, ConversationID, ResultCode).

items.title: failed_jobs table has no growth monitor — silent queue degradation
items.fix: Daily Schedule::call counting failed_jobs older than 24h; mail alert when threshold crossed.

items.title: Failed login attempts log only generic 'Invalid credentials'
items.fix: Resolve target user first; pass distinct reason codes (user_not_found, wrong_password, account_locked, email_not_verified).

items.title: AdminController::toggleUserStatus unaudited
items.fix: SecurityLogger custom event + Auditable on User. Reject toggles on landlords with active leases.

items.title: TenantController search/modalData endpoints rely on TenantScope without role guard
items.fix: Add role:landlord,caretaker to /tenants/search etc. Register TenantPolicy.

items.title: PDF download endpoints unthrottled — invoices.download, leases.download, ledger.pdf
items.fix: Apply throttle:export OR new pdf-render limiter (15/min/user).

items.title: REVERB_APP_SECRET=your-secret-key-here in committed .env.example
items.fix: Replace with empty values. Add deployment guard rejecting placeholder values in non-local envs.

items.title: DashboardService no duration instrumentation
items.fix: Wrap getLandlordDashboardData and helpers with hrtime() bracket; log to 'cache' channel with section/landlord_id/duration_ms/unit_count.

phase_1.title: Critical: dead-rule, mass-assign, SSRF, session-fixation, magic-link
phase_1.scope: CRYPTO-1, CRYPTO-2, CRYPTO-3, CRYPTO-5, PRIV-3, PRIV-4
phase_1.rationale: CRYPTO-1 means Phase-4 HANDLE-11 has zero runtime effect — single biggest gap. PRIV-4 is a silent role-grant failure. Other items are clear auth-bypass / SSRF vectors. All are small, focused fixes.

phase_2.title: Critical: cross-tenant write IDOR, caretaker bug, plaintext PII
phase_2.scope: PRIV-1, PRIV-2, PRIV-5, PRIV-6, CRYPTO-4, CRYPTO-6
phase_2.rationale: Direct cross-tenant write paths plus the caretaker auth()->id() bug class extension (4th occurrence). Bank PII at rest is compliance-fatal. APP_KEY rotation procedure is operational.

phase_3.title: Critical: rate-limit gaps for spam/DoS
phase_3.rationale: RATE-1 is a typo causing total lack of throttle. Others are direct spam/cost-amplification vectors against tenants.

phase_4.title: Critical: observability blackouts
phase_4.scope: OBS-1, OBS-2, OBS-3, OBS-4, OBS-6, OBS-11
phase_4.rationale: Sentry + meaningful health check + scheduler onFailure + NotificationService logging + PaymentConfiguration audit + metrics counters. Without any of these, money-loss events are invisible to ops.

phase_5.title: MEDIUM defense-in-depth
phase_5.scope: PRIV-7, PRIV-8, PRIV-9, PRIV-10, PRIV-11, PRIV-12, PRIV-13, RATE-6, RATE-7, RATE-8, RATE-9, RATE-10, RATE-11, CRYPTO-7, CRYPTO-8, CRYPTO-9, CRYPTO-10, CRYPTO-11, OBS-5, OBS-7, OBS-8, OBS-9, OBS-10, OBS-12, OBS-13, OBS-14

phase_6.title: LOW cleanup
phase_6.rationale: Pick up when touching the area.

out_of_scope: HIBP per-user rate — protected by login/register/password-reset limiters
Cache key collision via tenant.name — no instances found

commits.phases_1_through_6: Closed across 11 commits 80ec338..3e322eb (initial Phase-5 cycle, 50/54 findings) plus 3a579cf regression tests.

phase_5_cleanup.CRYPTO-11: 36fd2f1 — per-landlord encrypted webhook secrets on PaymentConfiguration with env fallback; BankWebhookController resolves landlord pre-validation.
phase_5_cleanup.PRIV-5 (M-Pesa half): 07e9c54 — MpesaWebhookController.findTenantByPhone now requires landlord_id resolved from BusinessShortCode; refuses cross-landlord match.
phase_5_cleanup.PRIV-12: 25c24c9 — TenantPolicy::view/viewLedger registered for User; six TenantController methods now use $this->authorize / Gate::denies.
phase_5_cleanup.OBS-1: c422057 — sentry/sentry-laravel installed; Integration::handles in bootstrap/app.php; DomainException::report forwards to Sentry::captureException.
